name: Martin Lefevre
occupation: Former customer data analyst / Currently unemployed
background: Worked at Synapse Telecom for five years on customer retention analytics and CRM reporting. He regularly handled bulk extracts for churn analysis, VIP complaint cases, and fraud review teams. He was dismissed on April 29, 2025 after an internal investigation found that he had copied work material onto a personal drive without authorization. He has no criminal record, but he has been under heavy financial pressure since losing his job and falling behind on loan payments.
personality: Controlled, articulate, and usually careful with his words. He tries to sound rational and wronged rather than defensive. When questioned about his dismissal, his resentment starts to show and he becomes noticeably less consistent.
hidden_truth: Martin did participate in the Synapse Telecom data theft as an insider accomplice. He did not perform the extraction himself, but he provided Antonin Morel with a current map of the CRM tables, details about which customer segments were most valuable, and guidance on a legacy service account that he believed was still active. He also explained which maintenance workstation was least likely to trigger attention overnight. Martin is trying to present himself as a disgruntled former employee with loose talk, not as someone who knowingly enabled a criminal breach.
strategy: Deny direct involvement in the breach. Admit he was angry at Synapse and may have complained to Antonin, but insist it was just talk. Downplay his technical knowledge and claim he had no way of knowing any old credentials still worked. If pressed on motive or money, become offended and pivot to how unfairly he was treated by management.
vulnerabilities: He knew the exact CRM tables and export process used in the theft
He was dismissed shortly before the breach and had a clear grievance against Synapse
Metadata links him to repeated encrypted messages with Antonin before the incident
He retained internal documentation after his departure when he should not have had it
His financial situation gives him a plausible motive to help sell stolen data
